name: em-dash-abuse
description: Excessive em-dash usage (3+ per paragraph) is a strong AI writing signal

name: let-me-starter
description: "Let me [verb]" sentence starters are characteristic of AI assistants

name: heres-the-thing
description: "Here's the thing/what" openers mimic conversational AI tone

name: binary-contrast
description: Binary contrast structures ("Not just X, but Y") are over-used by AI

name: wh-starters
pattern: ^\s*(what makes this|why this matters|why it matters|how this works|what this means|where this gets interesting)
description: Wh- question starters used as section headers are an AI pattern

name: bullet-overload
description: Long bullet-point lists (6+) without prose are a structural AI pattern

name: triple-adjective
description: Triple adjective chains before nouns are more common in AI writing

name: meta-reference
description: Self-referencing meta text is common in AI-generated content

name: passive-voice-density
description: Individual passive voice instance (aggregated for density check)

name: worth-noting
description: "It's worth noting" as a sentence opener is a strong AI signal

name: colon-list-intro
description: Colon followed by a list is a common AI formatting pattern

name: emoji-header
description: Emoji + bold header combinations are a strong AI formatting signal

name: number-list-pattern
description: Numbered lists with bold items are commonly AI-generated

name: tldr-pattern
description: TL;DR / key takeaway sections are AI structural patterns
